title: Resonate: Present Visual Stories that Transform Audiences
author: Nancy Duarte
description: Nancy Duarte, CEO of Duarte Inc., reveals the underlying story form of all great presentations — not just to make them more engaging, but to move audiences to action. Through analysis of 50+ speeches and TED talks, field research, and her decades of experience creating presentations for Fortune 500 leaders, Duarte identifies a consistent structure she calls the Presentation Form or Sparkline: an alternation between the dark of 'what is' and the light of 'what could be.' This rhythmic oscillation generates the tension that transforms information into story and audiences from passive listeners into active participants.
authorBio: Nancy Duarte is the CEO of Duarte, Inc., the largest design firm in Silicon Valley specializing in presentation design and visualization. She has been a leader in presentation design for over 30 years, creating visual stories for Fortune 500 companies, TED speakers, and executive leaders worldwide. Her firm designed the famous Al Gore 'An Inconvenient Truth' presentation. She is the author of slide:ology (2008), Resonate (2010), and The Visual Storyteller. Duarte has taught at Stanford, been featured in Harvard Business Review, Fast Company, and Wired, and her TEDx talk 'The Secret Structure of Great Talks' has been viewed millions of times. She founded the firm with her husband, Mark Duarte, in 1988.
